english: pf. bsnyegs (bsnyegs), fut. bsnyeg (bsnyeg), imp.snyog(s) (snyog(s)), W. *nyág-ce*, 1. c. accus. to hasten or run after, to pursue, frq.; also with rjés-nas (rjes nas), rjés-su (rjes su), rjés-bźin-du (rjes bzhin du), p̓yi-bźin-du (phyi bzhin du); raṅ-gró-sar snyogs (rang gro sar snyogs) hasten towards your aim! Mil; snyég-sar snyogs (snyeg sar snyogs) Lex. id.; bsnyég-tu (bsnyeg tu), or snyégs-su ₒdoṅ-ba (snyegs su 'dong ba) to walk hastily, to make haste or speed Dzl. — 2. to overtake, snyégs ma nús-pas (snyegs ma nus pas) not being able to reach Dzl. — 3. c. dat. to hasten to some place, ltád-mo-la (ltad mo la) to the play Mil.; to rise, ɣnám-la (gnam la) rising up to heaven, as a flame, Glr., a cedar Wdn., frq.; to strive or struggle for, to aspire to, nór-la (nor la) riches, sde-čén-la (sde chen la) increase of territory, źiṅ-k̓ams-bzáṅ-la (zhing khams bzang la) the region of eternal bliss. — snyég-ma (snyeg ma) pursuer Dzl.

1. inst. of rmyéṅ-ba (rmyeng ba), to stretch Mil. — 2. also snyéṅs-pa (snyengs pa), resp. for ₒjígs-pa ('jigs pa), vb. (pf. bsnyeṅs (bsnyengs), fut. bsnyeṅ (bsnyeng)) and sbst., rgyál-pos ma snyeṅs śig (rgyal pos ma snyengs shig) do not be afraid of the king! Dzl.

I. the crupper attached to a saddle Sch. II. = tsam (tsam): 1. ₒdi-snyéd (-čig) ('di snyed cig), de-snyéd (-čig) (de snyed cig) so much, so many, frq.; also for: how many! e.g. . . . yón-tan ₒdi-snyéd mṅao (yon tan 'di snyed mnga'o) how many excellent qualities has . . . ! Dzl.; či-snyéd (ci snyed), ji-snyéd (ji snyed) how much? how many? also snyed (snyed) alone (examples v. sub byé-ma (bye ma)). — 2. after round sums: about, near, stoṅ snyed (stong snyed), also stoṅ ji-snyéd (stong ji snyed) Mil. about a thousand.
Cs.: 1. to come or go near, to approach, gen. bsnyén-pa (bsnyen pa). — 2. to gain, to procure, inst. of rnyéd-pa (rnyed pa) (?).

1. vb., pf. bsnyems (bsnyems), to be proud or arrogant, to boast, ṅa-rgyál snyéms-pas (nga rgyal snyems pas) to be swollen with pride Dzl.; mťu-rtsál (mthu rtsal) (to be proud) of one's strength Dzl. — 2. sbst. pride, haughtiness, snyém-pa-čan (snyem pa can) prideful, proud, snyems-čúṅ (snyems chung) 1. prideless, humble, affable, kind, col. *nyom-čúṅ*, and *nyam-śúṅ*. 2. poor, indigent C.
or bsnyóg-pa (bsnyog pa), secondary form of snyég-pa (snyeg pa), esp. when signifying to wish earnestly, to crave for or lust after, also k̓a-snyóg-pa (kha snyog pa) Cs.

I. pf. bsnyad (bsnyad), 1. to draw out and twist, as in spinning Stg., C. — 2. Cs.: to tell, to relate, = snyád-pa (snyad pa). II. pf. bsnyod (bsnyod), bsnyos (bsnyos), fut. bsnyod (bsnyod), to feed, to give to eat and to drink, c.capir.

I. 1. pf. and fut. bsnyon (bsnyon), to deny, to disavow dishonestly, Dzl. 222, 2; 270, 8 to assert falsely, snyon byéd-pa (snyon byed pa) Glr. — 2. *nyon ḍú-če* W. is said to signify the same as *nyad ḍu-če* v. snyad (snyad). II. inst. of smyón-pa (smyon pa)
pf. bsnyabs (bsnyabs), fut. bsnyab (bsnyab) 1. to stretch out, e.g. the hand, Lex. — 2. W. to reach, by stretching one's self out, to arrive at, *nyob mi ťub*.

Lex. = तन्द्री I. 1. weariness, lassitude; laziness, idleness, lus snyoms-lči-ba yin (lus snyoms lci ba yin) one is exhausted and dull Med.; snyóms-la núl-ba (snyoms la nul ba) to be tired and exhausted. — snyóms-las (snyoms las) 1. indolence, unconcern, esp. religious indifference, Glr.; snyóms-las byéd-pa (snyoms las byed pa), or drán-pa (dran pa) Glr. to be lazy, indolent, indifferent; snyóms-las-čan (snyoms las can) adj. lazy etc. Glr. — 2. Sch.: an idle person(?). II. col., also Mil., inst. of snyems (snyems).

I. vb., pf. bsnyoms (bsnyoms), fut. bsnyom (bsnyom), to make even 1. to level, ɣnas (gnas) a place, Dzl.; sá-la snyóms-pa (sa la snyoms pa) to level with the ground, to demolish Dzl. — 2. p̓an-ťsun (phan tshun) to equalize different things, to arrange uniformly, zas (zas) one's meals, i.e. not cold and warm promiscuously Thgy.; to level, to reduce to an equality of condition, ltoɣs-p̓yug (ltogs phyug) rich and poor (according to the principles of the communists) Glr.; similarly mťo-dman (mtho dman) Tar. 74; ťams-čád-la snyóms-na bdag kyaṅ snyóms-par mdzad (thams cad la snyoms na bdag kyang snyoms par mdzad)
